title: PQC Today — Quick Overview
whatToExpect: A 6-minute tour of every PQC Today section
Pointers into the right module for your role and industry
Where to find authoritative standards (NIST, CCCS, ASD, ENISA)
What to read next once you've picked your persona

steps.title: Welcome to PQC Today
steps.whyItMatters: Sets the destination — a vendor-neutral resource for the post-quantum cryptography migration.
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe PQC Today in one sentence.
steps.narration: Welcome to PQC Today — a free, vendor-neutral resource for the post-quantum cryptography migration. We will tour every page in about six minutes.

cues.text: Welcome to PQC Today — vendor-neutral resource for the post-quantum migration.
We will tour every page in about six minutes.

steps.title: Persona picker — pick your role
steps.whyItMatters: The persona switcher tailors the entire app — modules, hints, journey steps, recommended actions — to your role, proficiency, industry, and region.
tasks: Pick a role that fits you
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to set your persona context.
steps.narration: The persona switcher in the top-right tailors every page to your role — executive, developer, architect, ops, researcher, or curious — plus your proficiency, industry, and region.

cues.text: Persona switcher — tailor every page to your role, proficiency, industry, and region.
Six roles: executive, developer, architect, ops, researcher, curious.

steps.title: Learn — modules and tutorials
steps.whyItMatters: Learn is the educational core: 40+ modules with interactive workshops you can run in your browser.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to find learning content.
steps.narration: Learn is the educational core. Forty-plus modules with interactive workshops you can run right in your browser.

cues.text: Learn — 40+ modules with interactive workshops in your browser.

steps.title: Walk PQC 101 — the foundation module
steps.whyItMatters: PQC 101 is the foundation module everyone starts with — Q-Day, Harvest-Now Decrypt-Later, the new NIST standards (FIPS 203 ML-KEM and FIPS 204 ML-DSA), the migration timeline, and what a ready org looks like.
tasks: Glance at the Visual + Workshop tabs
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe Q-Day, HNDL, and what FIPS 203/204 are in 60 seconds.
steps.narration: PQC 101 is the foundation module everyone starts with. Five sections: the quantum threat, the new algorithms, the migration timeline, the readiness picture, and the next steps. Six tabs surface the same content as text (Learn), diagrams (Visual), interactive demos (Workshop), short quizzes (Exercises), source documents (References), and vendor tooling (Tools & Products).

cues.text: PQC 101 — post-quantum cryptography in plain language - what it is, why your org cares
Section 1/5: Why Pqc
Section 2/5: Whats Changing
Section 3/5: The Timeline
Section 4/5: Who Acts
Section 5/5: Next Steps
Workshop 1/5: Why Pqc
Workshop 2/5: Whats Changing
Workshop 3/5: The Timeline
Workshop 4/5: Who Acts
Workshop 5/5: Next Steps
Visual tab — diagrams and animations for this topic.
Exercises — short knowledge checks to confirm you got it.
References — primary sources every claim is cited from.
Tools & Products — vendor tooling and PQC-ready software.

steps.title: Role-tailored modules
steps.whyItMatters: Once you set a persona, the Learn page surfaces modules calibrated to your role — executive deep-dives, developer crypto APIs, architect migration patterns, etc.
tasks: Note the persona-recommended modules near the top
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe how Learn personalises by role.
steps.narration: Once you have set a persona, Learn surfaces modules calibrated to your role — exec deep dives, developer APIs, architect migration patterns, ops cert lifecycle.

cues.text: Role-tailored modules surface based on your active persona.

steps.title: Workshop tooling — interactive crypto
steps.whyItMatters: Each Learn module has a Workshop tab where you run real cryptographic operations — generate keys, sign messages, verify certs — entirely in your browser.
steps.expectedOutput: You see real crypto operations running in the browser.
steps.narration: Each module has a Workshop tab. Real cryptographic operations — keygen, signing, verification — run in your browser using OpenSSL WASM and liboqs.

cues.text: Workshop tab — real keygen, signing, verification in the browser.

steps.title: Quiz — test your understanding
steps.whyItMatters: After modules, the quiz validates that you can answer the most-asked PQC questions at boardroom or interview speed.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to validate your understanding.
steps.narration: After modules, the Quiz validates that you can answer the most-asked PQC questions at boardroom or interview speed.

cues.text: Quiz — validate your understanding at interview / boardroom speed.

steps.title: Timeline — global migration roadmap
steps.whyItMatters: Timeline tracks every PQC mandate, advisory, and milestone from 2020 to 2035 — country by country, agency by agency.
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe the timeline structure.
steps.narration: Timeline tracks every PQC mandate, advisory, and milestone from 2020 to 2035, organised by country and agency.

cues.text: Timeline — every PQC mandate and milestone, 2020 to 2035, by country.

steps.title: Country deadlines — when binding
steps.whyItMatters: The Gantt rows make it clear when each country's deadline binds — ASD ISM-1917 by 2030, CCCS ITSM phased 2026/2031/2035, NIST IR 8547 deprecation by 2030/2035.
steps.expectedOutput: You can identify the binding deadline for any major jurisdiction.
steps.narration: The Gantt rows make it clear when each country's deadlines bind — ASD by 2030, CCCS phased through 2035, NIST staged deprecation.

cues.text: Country deadlines — ASD 2030 · CCCS 2026/2031/2035 · NIST 2030/2035.

steps.title: Country left rail — quick navigation
steps.whyItMatters: The left rail jumps to a country's row without scrolling. Bookmark countries you track for the My Timeline view.
steps.expectedOutput: You can navigate to any country's deadlines in one click.
steps.narration: The left rail jumps you to a specific country's row. Bookmark the ones you track to build a personal timeline.

cues.text: Left rail — single-click navigation to any country's row.

steps.title: Algorithms — the PQC catalogue
steps.whyItMatters: Algorithms compares every standardised and candidate post-quantum algorithm — ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, FrodoKEM, HQC, Classic McEliece, and more.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to compare PQC algorithms.
steps.narration: Algorithms is the technical reference — every standardised and candidate post-quantum scheme, with key sizes, security levels, and standardisation status.

cues.text: Algorithms — every standardised and candidate PQC scheme, with sizes and status.

steps.title: FIPS picks — ML-KEM and ML-DSA
steps.whyItMatters: ML-KEM-768 and ML-DSA-65 are the two production-ready picks for most workloads — these replace RSA key exchange and ECDSA signatures.
steps.expectedOutput: You can name the two production-ready PQC picks.
steps.narration: For most workloads the production-ready picks are ML-KEM-768 — replacing RSA key exchange — and ML-DSA-65 — replacing ECDSA signatures.

cues.text: ML-KEM-768 replaces RSA key exchange. ML-DSA-65 replaces ECDSA signatures.

steps.title: Transition tab — classical to PQC mapping
steps.whyItMatters: The Transition tab maps every classical algorithm to its post-quantum successor — invaluable when scoping a migration.
steps.expectedOutput: You can find the PQC successor for any classical algorithm in your stack.
steps.narration: The Transition tab maps every classical algorithm to its post-quantum successor — RSA, ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA — so you can scope a migration without manual research.

cues.text: Transition tab — classical → PQC mapping for RSA, ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA, etc.

steps.title: Migrate — 700+ products tracked
steps.whyItMatters: Migrate is the buyer's guide — 700+ products across cloud, hardware, identity, and crypto libraries, each with vendor PQC roadmap and certification status.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to look for vendor PQC readiness.
steps.narration: Migrate is the buyer's guide. Seven hundred-plus products across cloud, hardware, identity, and crypto libraries, with each vendor's PQC roadmap and certification status.

cues.text: Migrate — 700+ products, each with vendor PQC roadmap and certifications.

steps.title: Filter by layer and vendor
steps.whyItMatters: Layer + vendor filters cut the 700-row list to the products you actually use — cloud, app servers, hardware, libraries, etc.
steps.expectedOutput: You can locate any vendor's product in seconds.
steps.narration: Layer and vendor filters cut the catalogue to the products you actually run.

cues.text: Layer + vendor filters — cut to the products you actually use.

steps.title: Product detail — cert status and migration steps
steps.whyItMatters: Each product card shows the vendor's PQC roadmap, NIST validation status (FIPS 140-3, ACVP, CC), and concrete migration steps.
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe a vendor's PQC readiness without leaving the page.
steps.narration: Each product card shows the vendor's roadmap, NIST validation status — FIPS 140-3, ACVP, Common Criteria — and concrete migration steps.

cues.text: Product detail — roadmap, FIPS 140-3, ACVP, CC status, migration steps.

steps.title: Compliance — frameworks and deadlines
steps.whyItMatters: Compliance maps every framework that governs cryptographic adoption: NIST CNSA 2.0, FIPS 140-3, CCCS ITSM, ASD ISM, PCI DSS, SWIFT CSP, G7 CEG.
steps.expectedOutput: You know which frameworks govern your jurisdiction.
steps.narration: Compliance maps every framework — NIST, FIPS, CCCS, ASD, PCI, SWIFT, G7 — with required algorithms and binding dates.

cues.text: Compliance — every framework with required algorithms and binding dates.

steps.title: Compliance tabs — Frameworks · Standards · Cert Records
steps.whyItMatters: The four compliance tabs separate technical standards, compliance frameworks, certification records (CMVP, ACVP, CC), and the CSWP-39 framework.
steps.expectedOutput: You know how compliance content is organised.
steps.narration: Four tabs: Technical Standards, Compliance Frameworks, Cert Records, CSWP-39. Each is filterable by region and industry.

cues.text: Tabs: Technical Standards · Compliance · Cert Records · CSWP-39.

steps.title: Region filter — your jurisdiction
steps.whyItMatters: Filter by your region to see only the frameworks that apply to you — saves you wading through global content.
steps.expectedOutput: You can isolate frameworks for your jurisdiction.
steps.narration: Filter by region — Americas, EU, Asia-Pacific, or your specific country — to see only the frameworks that apply to you.

cues.text: Region filter — isolate frameworks for your jurisdiction.

steps.title: Threats — quantum risk by industry
steps.whyItMatters: Threats catalogues concrete quantum risks tied to specific industries — finance, healthcare, energy, telecom, etc.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to find sector-specific threats.
steps.narration: Threats catalogues concrete quantum risks by industry — what is at risk, what cryptography it relies on, what NIST recommends as the replacement.

cues.text: Threats — quantum risks by industry, with severity and primary sources.

steps.title: Threat left rail — jump to any threat
steps.whyItMatters: The left rail groups threats by industry and lets you jump to a specific one — FIN-001 Project Leap, CLOUD-004 HSM gap, etc.
tasks: Click a threat in the rail
steps.expectedOutput: You can locate any threat in two clicks.
steps.narration: The left rail groups threats by industry. Click any item to jump to its card.

cues.text: Left rail — threats grouped by industry, click to jump.

steps.title: Bookmarks and shareable threats
steps.whyItMatters: Bookmark threats to build your watch-list. Each threat has a shareable URL you can drop into Slack, email, or board materials.
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to save and share a threat.
steps.narration: Bookmark threats to build a watch-list. Each threat has a shareable URL for Slack, email, or board materials.

cues.text: Bookmark + share URLs for every threat.

steps.title: Library — the citation desk
steps.whyItMatters: Library aggregates every primary source: NIST FIPS, IETF RFCs, agency guidance from CCCS, ASD, ENISA, plus academic papers.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to source authoritative PQC documents.
steps.narration: Library is your citation desk. NIST FIPS, IETF RFCs, agency guidance from CCCS, ASD, ENISA, plus the academic papers behind every algorithm.

cues.text: Library — NIST FIPS, IETF RFCs, agency guidance, academic papers.

steps.title: Bookmark and cite
steps.whyItMatters: Bookmark NIST FIPS 203, FIPS 204, NIST IR 8547 — the documents you'll cite the most. The bookmark surface follows you across the app.
steps.expectedOutput: You can cite the two NIST PQC standards from memory.
steps.narration: Bookmark FIPS 203, FIPS 204, and NIST IR 8547 — the deprecation calendar. The bookmark surface follows you everywhere.

cues.text: Bookmark FIPS 203, 204, and NIST IR 8547 — the most-cited docs.

steps.title: Leaders — who's driving PQC
steps.whyItMatters: Leaders profiles the academics, industry adopters, and government officials shaping the migration. Useful when you need a quotable name.
steps.expectedOutput: You can name a few PQC leaders relevant to your context.
steps.narration: Leaders profiles the people driving the post-quantum migration — academics, industry adopters, government officials.

cues.text: Leaders — academics, industry adopters, government officials.

steps.title: Filter by category and region
steps.whyItMatters: Filter by category — Algorithm Inventor, Industry Adopter, Government, Standards — and by region to find peers in your context.
steps.expectedOutput: You can find any leader in two filters.
steps.narration: Filter by category — Inventor, Adopter, Government, Standards — and by region to find peers in your jurisdiction.

cues.text: Filters: category (Inventor / Adopter / Government / Standards) + region.

steps.title: Changelog — what's new
steps.whyItMatters: The Changelog page shows every release — new modules, data updates, security fixes — so you know when to revisit.
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to track what is new in PQC Today.
steps.narration: The Changelog tracks every release. New modules, data updates, security fixes — so you know when to revisit.

cues.text: Changelog — track every release: new modules, data, fixes.

steps.title: About — how the platform works
steps.whyItMatters: About is the trust page. It answers where the data comes from, who maintains it, and what stays in your browser.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to verify the platform's bona fides.
steps.narration: About is the trust page — answers the questions any CISO will ask before you share content from this app.

cues.text: About — the trust page. CISO questions answered.

steps.title: PQC Today mission and vision
steps.whyItMatters: The Vision section explains why PQC Today exists: a vendor-neutral, GPL-licensed reference for the whole post-quantum migration.
steps.expectedOutput: You can articulate the PQC Today mission.
steps.narration: The Vision section explains the mission — vendor-neutral, GPL-licensed reference for the whole post-quantum migration. No paywalls, no signups required.

cues.text: Mission — vendor-neutral, GPL-licensed, no paywalls, no signups.

steps.title: Data Privacy — what stays in your browser
steps.whyItMatters: Data Privacy explains the privacy model: nothing leaves your browser unless you opt in. All assessment data is local-first.
steps.expectedOutput: You can explain the privacy model to your security team.
steps.narration: Data Privacy — nothing leaves your browser unless you opt in. All assessment data is local-first, stored in localStorage.

cues.text: Data Privacy — nothing leaves your browser unless you opt in.

steps.title: Security Audit — the platform itself reviewed
steps.whyItMatters: Security Audit shows the platform itself has been reviewed — OWASP Top 10 controls, no remote code execution, CSP enforced.
steps.expectedOutput: You can answer 'is this safe to use' for your team.
steps.narration: Security Audit shows the platform's own controls — OWASP Top 10 enforced, no remote code execution, Content Security Policy active.

cues.text: Security Audit — OWASP Top 10 controls, no RCE, CSP active.

steps.title: License + SBOM
steps.whyItMatters: License (GPL-3.0) and SBOM (every dependency listed) cover compliance — what your legal team needs to clear use.
steps.expectedOutput: You know the licensing and dependency provenance.
steps.narration: License is GPL-3.0. The SBOM lists every dependency the app uses. Your legal team can clear it in minutes.

cues.text: License GPL-3.0 · SBOM lists every dependency · legal-clearable in minutes.

steps.title: Community feed — follow updates
steps.whyItMatters: The community section lists where to follow PQC Today updates — GitHub releases, the changelog page, and contributor channels.
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to keep up with new content and standards updates.
steps.narration: Community lists where to follow updates — GitHub releases, the changelog, and how to contribute back if you spot a gap.

cues.text: Community — GitHub, changelog, how to contribute.

steps.title: Pick a persona for the tailored workshop
steps.whyItMatters: The generic tour shows what is available. A persona-specific workshop tells you what to do — pick yours and re-open the Workshop tab.
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to switch from generic overview to a persona-specific workshop.
steps.narration: You have seen the surfaces. Pick a persona — Executive, Developer, Architect, Ops, Researcher, or Curious — and the Workshop tab will surface a flow tailored to your role.

cues.text: Pick a persona for a tailored workshop. Welcome to PQC Today.
